mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously whenever rate trajectories are observable.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% provides baseline for rate decline measurement
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.1% establishes baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment deterioration signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations. This interaction effect produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days because both demand components must deteriorate simultaneously to trigger the signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 723853M and HOUST at 1366K provide baseline for decline measurement
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the correlation between joint RSAFS-HOUST deterioration and negative SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below its 12-month moving average, signaling supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and reduces earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets rapidly reprrice supply constraints.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.511 (index 2017=100) provides current level for MA comparison
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO MA crossovers are not significantly negative (mean return > 0 with t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month, signaling labor market cooling that reduces household income growth and consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159218K provides baseline for growth rate calculation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following payroll deceleration signals are not significantly negative (mean return > 0 with t-test p > 0.10), or the signal fails to predict labor market deterioration within the subsequent 2-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month moving average, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 319.775 establish baseline for restrictive regime detection
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or the joint effect of high rates and elevated inflation does not produce significantly lower returns than either condition alone (interaction p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings growth and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 23536.293B (chained 2017 dollars) provides baseline for growth calculation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDP deceleration signals are not significantly negative (mean return > 0 with t-test p > 0.10), or the signal fails to predict subsequent economic weakness within the 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above their 6-month moving average while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow above their 3-month trend, signaling robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST above 6-month MA AND RSAFS above 3-month trend
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1366K and RSAFS at 723853M provide baseline for strength detection
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with strong demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or the joint demand strength signal does not produce significantly higher returns than either component alone (interaction p > 0.15).
